title: Comprehensive of RPC Protocol: Technical Specifications, Threats, Attack and Defense For Red and Blue
author: Mohammad Ali Pour, Fazel
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: This article provides an in-depth exploration of the Remote Procedure Call (RPC) protocol, a fundamental component in distributed computing systems. It covers the technical specifications of RPC, its integration with MS-RPC in Windows, and the attack surfaces exploited by Advanced Persistent Threat (APT) groups. Additionally, the paper discusses tools and techniques used for exploitation, alongside detection and mitigation strategies to enhance security posture. Designed for red and blue teams, the article combines technical insights with practical approaches to address RPC-related security challenges.
